message:
feat(simd_scan): add string-phase state machine for OSC/DCS/SOS/PM/APC byte counting (ft-nk6u9)

The four scan variants in simd_scan.rs (scan_newlines_and_ansi plus three
variants for chunked input) previously treated the C1 string-introducer
controls (OSC ']', DCS 'P', SOS 'X', PM '^', APC '_') as CSI-style
terminators. That under-counted the ANSI byte total by the entire string
body: an OSC-0 title-set sequence `ESC ] 0 ; TITLE BEL` would count only
the leading 4 bytes (ESC ] 0 ;) as "ANSI" and ignore the title payload +
BEL terminator, skewing every downstream heuristic that used the ANSI
density to decide whether a chunk was "display-heavy".

## Solution: add a proper string-phase state machine

### New StringPhase enum

  - None   — not inside a string-introducer body
  - Body   — inside OSC/DCS/SOS/PM/APC, waiting for BEL or ESC\
  - SawEsc — saw ESC inside a string body; next byte decides whether
             this is the `\` of ST (end of string) or another embedded
             sequence

Default is StringPhase::None so OutputScanState::default() behaves
identically to the pre-fix state — zero migration burden for callers
that never hit a string sequence.

### OutputScanState carries string_phase alongside in_escape

Add `string_phase: StringPhase` field to OutputScanState so cross-chunk
callers (the three streaming variants) can correctly resume mid-string
across chunk boundaries. A string body that spans chunks no longer gets
truncated at the boundary; instead, the Body phase persists and body
bytes continue to count in the next chunk.

### Unified ansi_state_step helper

Extract the shared state-machine logic into
`ansi_state_step(b, &mut in_escape, &mut string_phase) -> bool` so all
four scan variants use the same transitions. Returns true iff the byte
counts as ANSI.

Full state transition table (preserved verbatim from the inline doc
comment for clarity; this is the contract the four variants rely on):

  * ESC always counts; enters "in escape" state.
  * While in escape, NOT yet in a string:
    - `[` (CSI intro) → stay in escape
    - One of `] P X ^ _` → enter string Body, stay in escape
    - Byte in 0x40..=0x7E → single-char C1 final, exit escape
    - Byte < 0x40 (parameter/intermediate) → stay in escape
    - Byte > 0x7E (non-ASCII) → exit escape (best-effort)
  * While in Body:
    - BEL (0x07) → exit escape+string
    - ESC → enter SawEsc, stay in escape+string
    - else → body byte (counted, phase unchanged)
  * While in SawEsc:
    - `\` (0x5C) → String Terminator, exit escape+string
    - ESC → stay in SawEsc (another ESC, still waiting for `\`)
    - else → drop back to Body (ESC was part of body)

### is_string_intro_byte(b) helper

Inline helper matching against 0x50/0x58/0x5D/0x5E/0x5F (P/X/]/^/_).
Kept as a pub(crate) function rather than inlined so future callers
(e.g. a future DCS-aware path) can reuse the canonical set.

## Beads

- Close ft-8mgti with reason "landed in 98f5360a: KittyImage::
  TransmitDataAndDisplay now formats with action T to match parse_apc,
  plus a display->parse regression test in apc.rs"
- Close ft-atcr0 with reason "Landed in 5eb9f956 (fix(apc,
  backpressure, ingest): NaN-proof backpressure)"

Tracks ft-nk6u9. Follow-on work: wire the new ansi_state_step into the
four scan variants + regression tests asserting byte counts against
fixture string sequences. This commit lands the machinery; variant
migration is the next commit.

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>

@@ -34,7 +34,7 @@
 {"id":"ft-7ojbj","title":"rate_limit_tracker: parse_retry_after can produce Duration that panics Instant + cooldown","description":"# Defect

`crates/frankenterm-core/src/rate_limit_tracker.rs:441-473` `parse_retry_after` has no upper bound on the parsed duration.

Plain-number path at :452-454:
```rust
if let Ok(secs) = text.parse::<u64>() {
    return Some(Duration::from_secs(secs));
}
```

'N unit' path at :458-469 has a `checked_mul` guard, but the plain number path returns `Duration::from_secs(u64::MAX)` untouched (u64::MAX secs ≈ 584 billion years).

The returned Duration flows into `PaneRateLimitState::record_event` at :164:
```rust
let expiry = event.detected_at + event.cooldown;
```

`Instant + Duration` panics when the sum overflows the platform's underlying representation. On macOS/Linux Instant is backed by clock_gettime timespec/uptime — adding ~584B years to a fresh Instant will overflow.

# Reachability

`retry_after_text` reaches record_event from pattern detection output. `trigger_retry_after` (workflows/builtin_workflows.rs) extracts the string from agent-emitted rate-limit messages. A malformed provider response or adversarial agent output ('Retry-After: 18446744073709551615') would panic the tracker on record.

# Existing tests

`parse_retry_after_overflow_returns_none` at :515-518 covers the 'N unit' checked_mul path with 'u64::MAX hours', but NOT the plain-number path with 'u64::MAX'. That path currently returns `Some` unclamped.

# Fix

Clamp parse_retry_after to a sane max (24h ceiling — legitimate provider Retry-After values never exceed 1h in practice, and any longer wait is a signal to bail rather than block the tracker). Apply the cap uniformly to plain-number and 'N unit' paths. Also clamp DEFAULT_COOLDOWN_SECS fallback if it ever grows.

Add regression test: parse_retry_after_plain_u64_max_is_clamped asserts the plain-number path returns a Duration ≤ the cap, not None and not u64::MAX.

# Severity

MEDIUM — requires adversarial/malformed provider input to trigger. Tracker is behind rate_limit_tracker.lock() in workflows/builtin_workflows.rs so the panic takes down the workflow task but is scoped to one pane's rate-limit processing. Fix is trivial: one `.min(MAX_COOLDOWN)` call plus one test.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-22T09:40:08.211722Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-22T09:50:10.213965Z","closed_at":"2026-04-22T09:50:10.213664Z","close_reason":"Landed in 7998d1a4 (fix(rate_limit_tracker): clamp parsed Retry-After at MAX_COOLDOWN_SECS)","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"ft-84ulk","title":"workflow runner releases pane lock on pre-start cx cancellation","status":"in_progress","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","assignee":"pane8","created_at":"2026-04-22T10:07:27.230187Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-22T10:07:37.820310Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"ft-8jbpj","title":"wezterm.rs pre-existing compile breakage blocks ft-e34d9.10.5.2","description":"While validating ft-e34d9.10.5.2 on 2026-04-21, the checkout surfaced pre-existing source breakage already present in HEAD.

Primary blocker:
- crates/frankenterm-core/src/wezterm.rs:1963-1968 contains a malformed pool_result branch. After the explicit-Cx else arm calls pool.send_paste_with_cx(&cx, pane_id, data).await, HEAD still has a stray pool.send_paste(pane_id, data).await line before the branch closes. That leaves the control-flow expression malformed and blocks frankenterm-core compile/hygiene in the vendored mux verification lane.

Related hygiene blocker surfaced in the same lane:
- crates/frankenterm-core/src/vendored/mux_client.rs:1891 contains a stray ); inside PaneOutputSubscription, so rustfmt cannot parse the file while formatting touched vendored mux changes.

Evidence:
- dirty diff against HEAD shows removing the stray send_paste line in wezterm.rs and the stray ); in mux_client.rs restores parsable source shape locally.
- rch exec -- env CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/tmp/ft-p7-orch-target cargo test -p frankenterm-core --lib cancellation_during_ --features asupersync-runtime,vendored -- --nocapture surfaced pre-existing frankenterm-core compile failures during the same validation lane.

This bug is blocking clean verification progress on ft-e34d9.10.5.2 support work and should be fixed before further vendored mux migration validation claims are made.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-21T16:59:15.535866Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-21T18:01:59.556560Z","closed_at":"2026-04-21T18:01:59.556225Z","close_reason":"Fixed by 9e1bd63b (ft-xbnl0.2.5 bulk cfg strip) — stray pool.send_paste and mux_client.rs stray ); both removed with the non-cx fallback branches","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}

 {"id":"ft-8wt4o","title":"[gap] README runtime_compat quarantine claim not matched by frankenterm-core product paths","description":"README.md:41 claims only the remaining watch-daemon/runtime startup path still crosses `runtime_compat`; grep shows live product modules still depend on `runtime_compat` in crates/frankenterm-core/src/snapshot_engine.rs:37, crates/frankenterm-core/src/storage.rs:5918, crates/frankenterm-core/src/event_stream.rs:599, crates/frankenterm-core/src/distributed.rs:1037, and crates/frankenterm-core/src/vendored/mux_client.rs:15,308.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"docs","created_at":"2026-04-21T17:34:15.760863Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-21T18:02:27.754838Z","closed_at":"2026-04-21T18:02:27.754618Z","close_reason":"Fixed in af925d26 — README runtime_compat boundary claim corrected to acknowledge multiple production seams","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"ft-akx00","title":"[EPIC] Eliminate verified first-party placeholders and stubs","description":"# Goal
Eliminate every verified first-party placeholder, stub, mock-like runtime branch, and misleading partial implementation surfaced by the 2026-04-02 audit.

Every in-scope item must end in exactly one of two states:
1. Real shipping behavior with tests, metrics, and operator-visible semantics.
2. Explicitly narrowed scope backed by code changes, docs, and guardrails so the code no longer masquerades as implemented behavior.

# Inventory Covered By This Epic
- CLI / operator support: `build_ntm_stub_response`, `chrono_stub_now`, scheduled backup compression fallback.
- Support crates: `frankenterm/env-bootstrap` and `ringlog` bootstrap/logger stubs.
- Client/runtime control plane: `ActiveWorkspaceChanged` TODO, unilateral-message FIXME, `spawn_pane` unimplemented, alt-screen hardcoded false.
- SDK / connectors / search: transparency verification stub, certification integration probe stub, generated Rust SDK transport unimplemented, `CrossEncoderReranker` no-op, `Model2VecEmbedder` stub, FastEmbed sentinel constructor behavior.
- Core runtime / UX: `NullPaneScrollbackAccess`, FTUI placeholder views, recording delta encodings, stale PaneCapabilities stub annotation.
- Rendering / windowing: Wayland seat+keyboard TODOs, WGL/macOS/EGL resize gaps, bitmap/WebGPU readback gaps, terminfo raw/cropped image gaps, GUI background/glyph placeholders.
- Mux / SSH: tmux PTY lifecycle gaps, detach unimplemented across domains, SFTP metadata mutation, SSH `Match Exec` evaluation.

# Scope Boundary
Only first-party source is in scope. Test doubles under `#[cfg(test)]`, intentional null objects, unsupported-platform no-op shims, and vendored upstream TODOs are handled only if this program reclassifies them with evidence.","design":"# Execution Strategy
1. Start with inventory discipline so no suspect disappears mid-stream.
2. Attack operator-visible and machine-control surfaces first; silent stubs in control-plane APIs are the highest trust risk.
3. Replace silent pass-through behavior with either real implementations or explicit hard failures with actionable errors while work is underway.
4. Prefer shared primitives over leaf fixes. Example: alt-screen truth should come from a real pane-status pipeline, not per-call heuristics.
5. Use the no-backwards-compatibility policy aggressively: if a placeholder path should not exist, remove or collapse it instead of preserving a fake API.
6. Every completed child bead must remove the misleading placeholder signal from code and add regression tests proving the real behavior.","acceptance_criteria":"- Every verified suspect from the audit is mapped to a child bead under this epic.
- No in-scope placeholder remains in a shipping path without either a real implementation or an explicit scoped declassification.
- Comments/docs that still say "stub", "TODO", or "not implemented" on completed paths are removed or rewritten.
- Regression tests exist for each completed surface, with supported-platform behavior verified where applicable.
- A final closure comment cross-checks the audit inventory against the completed child beads with no orphaned items.
- Every sub-epic and concrete task names required unit-test coverage, required end-to-end or explicitly assigned integration coverage, and structured logging/artifact outputs before closure.
- Failure artifacts are sufficient to diagnose the issue without rerunning locally: stdout/stderr, structured logs, fixture or payload diffs, and screenshots or pixel diffs where rendering is involved.","notes":"Out-of-scope unless reclassified by evidence: test-only fake panes, intentional null-object helpers like `NoopCaptureSink`, unsupported-platform `Option::None` style shims, and vendored/upstream dependency TODOs.

This epic is intentionally structured by architectural seams rather than one bead per grep hit. The goal is to fix root causes and shared primitives, not just delete placeholder strings.

## Verification Doctrine
- Follow the shared verification contract in `ft-akx00.8`.
- Heavy compile/test/clippy/benchmark sweeps for this program must run via `rch exec -- ...`.
- Completion comments must record exact commands, worker/environment, and artifact locations.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2026-04-03T00:27:33.773255Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-19T15:56:02.501104Z","closed_at":"2026-04-19T15:56:02.500840Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["e2e-tests","integration-tests","placeholder-remediation","quality-gate","rendering","stub-audit","test-coverage","test-logging","user-impact"],"comments":[{"id":2740,"issue_id":"ft-akx00","author":"jemanuel","text":"This epic is the execution wrapper for the 2026-04-02 placeholder/stub audit. The child tracks are ordered to minimize wasted work:
- machine-control and client behavior first,
- search/connector trust next,
- core runtime and FTUI after shared state surfaces are real,
- rendering/backend parity after higher-risk control paths,
- mux/SSH operational gaps in parallel where isolated.

Future-self rule: if a new suspect appears while working these beads, either attach it to the appropriate child bead or create a new child under this epic immediately. Do not let the inventory drift back into ad hoc comments.","created_at":"2026-04-03T00:28:55Z"},{"id":3545,"issue_id":"ft-akx00","author":"jemanuel","text":"Closure ledger: All 8 children closed. ft-akx00.1 (closure ledger), ft-akx00.2 (machine-control/SDK), ft-akx00.3 (operator-support/persistence), ft-akx00.4 (connector/search), ft-akx00.5 (runtime/scrollback/recording/FTUI), ft-akx00.6 (rendering/window backends), ft-akx00.7 (tmux/mux-domain/SSH), ft-akx00.8 (shared verification contract). Full placeholder-remediation program complete.","created_at":"2026-04-19T15:55:59Z"}]}
@@ -71,7 +71,7 @@
 {"id":"ft-akx00.7.4","title":"Implement SSH Match Exec evaluation in config loading","description":"`frankenterm/ssh/src/config.rs` still leaves SSH `Match Exec` criteria unimplemented.

This task owns deciding and implementing the project’s real behavior for `Match Exec` so SSH config parsing no longer silently omits an advertised criterion.","design":"1. Confirm the desired compatibility target for SSH config semantics in this project.
2. Implement `Match Exec` evaluation with clear execution, quoting, and error rules.
3. Harden the design against obvious safety pitfalls from arbitrary command execution.
4. Add tests for matching, non-matching, command failure, and disabled-policy cases.","acceptance_criteria":"- `Match Exec` is no longer a placeholder omission.
- The config loader documents the exact supported semantics and any safety restrictions.
- Tests cover success, mismatch, failure, and disabled/denied execution modes.
- Callers can tell whether a config line was unsupported, denied, or simply evaluated false.
- Comprehensive unit tests cover the happy path, edge/boundary cases, explicit error/failure/cancellation modes, and repeat-run stability relevant to this bead.
- A dedicated E2E/integration or explicitly named higher-level suite exercises the user-facing or integration-facing surface touched by this bead, with structured logs, artifacts, and exact execution commands captured under the shared contract in `ft-akx00.8`.","notes":"Be explicit about security posture here. If command execution must be gated or disabled by policy, document and implement that as part of the feature rather than bolting it on later.

## Verification Baseline
- Follow the shared verification and artifact contract in `ft-akx00.8`.
- Record exact commands, artifact paths, and if applicable `rch` worker identity in the completion evidence for this bead.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"SnowyCastle","created_at":"2026-04-03T00:32:27.135986Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-03T20:52:53.816310Z","closed_at":"2026-04-03T20:52:53.816104Z","close_reason":"Completed","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["e2e-tests","integration-tests","placeholder-remediation","quality-gate","stub-audit","test-coverage","test-logging","user-impact"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"ft-akx00.7.4","depends_on_id":"ft-akx00.7","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-04-03T00:32:27.135986Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":2771,"issue_id":"ft-akx00.7.4","author":"jemanuel","text":"`Match Exec` is one of those features where naïve compatibility can create security regret. The clever implementation is the one that is both honest and safe.","created_at":"2026-04-03T00:32:27Z"},{"id":2797,"issue_id":"ft-akx00.7.4","author":"jemanuel","text":"## Detailed Verification Plan
- Proposed unit tests:
  - parsing and evaluation of `Match Exec`
  - quoting, exit-code semantics, and disabled-policy behavior
- Proposed E2E script: `scripts/e2e_ssh_match_exec.sh`
  - run config fixtures with allowed, failing, and denied `Match Exec` commands
- Required logs/artifacts:
  - match clause id, command executed or denied, exit code, policy gate result, config fixture snapshot","created_at":"2026-04-03T02:04:52Z"}]}
 {"id":"ft-akx00.8","title":"Define shared verification, E2E, and artifact contract for placeholder-remediation work","description":"Define the shared verification contract for the entire `ft-akx00` program so implementers do not improvise test coverage, logging shape, artifact capture, or execution evidence on a bead-by-bead basis.

This bead exists because placeholder-remediation is high risk: many changes touch trust-sensitive control paths, platform backends, or user-facing UX surfaces. We want one reusable contract for proof, not 24 inconsistent interpretations of “tested”.","design":"1. Define the minimum unit-test standard for this program: happy path, edge/boundary behavior, explicit failure/error/cancellation modes, and repeat-run stability/idempotence where relevant.
2. Define the E2E/integration contract: each concrete bead must land either a dedicated script or an explicitly named extension to an existing shared suite.
3. Define the structured logging schema for verification runs. Minimum fields: `bead_id`, `scenario_id`, `surface`, `step`, `status`, `duration_ms`, `correlation_id`, `backend`, `platform`, `artifact_dir`, and redaction markers.
4. Define the artifact manifest for every verification run. Minimum files: `commands.txt`, `env.txt`, `structured.log`, `stdout.txt`, `stderr.txt`, `summary.json`, plus surface-specific artifacts like screenshots, pixel diffs, JSON payload snapshots, or remote traces.
5. Define naming conventions for new E2E scripts and artifact directories so failures are easy to browse.
6. Require heavy compile/test/clippy/benchmark workloads to run via `rch exec -- ...`, with worker identity and command transcript captured in evidence.","acceptance_criteria":"- The shared verification contract is explicit enough that every concrete `ft-akx00.*.*` task can reference it without ambiguity.
- Proposed E2E suite names and artifact directory conventions exist for every track in the program.
- The contract includes structured logging, artifact capture, redaction, and reproduction-command requirements.
- The contract is reflected back into the existing `ft-akx00` epics/tasks via updated acceptance criteria, notes, or comments.","notes":"Reopened: stalled 13-22 days with no recent activity. Prior claimant likely inactive.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"SunnyTiger","created_at":"2026-04-03T02:04:25.228541Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-19T14:34:16.480153Z","closed_at":"2026-04-19T14:34:16.479984Z","close_reason":"Completed shared verification contract","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["e2e-tests","integration-tests","placeholder-remediation","quality-gate","stub-audit","test-coverage","test-logging","user-impact"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"ft-akx00.8","depends_on_id":"ft-akx00","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-04-03T02:04:25.228541Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":2773,"issue_id":"ft-akx00.8","author":"jemanuel","text":"Suggested suite map for future implementers:
- machine-control/client/sdk -> `scripts/e2e_machine_control_*.sh`
- operator/startup/backup -> `scripts/e2e_ops_*.sh`
- connector/search -> `scripts/e2e_search_*.sh` and `scripts/e2e_connector_*.sh`
- core/FTUI -> `scripts/e2e_core_*.sh` and TUI snapshot fixtures
- rendering -> `scripts/e2e_render_*.sh` plus screenshot/pixel-diff artifacts
- mux/SSH -> `scripts/e2e_mux_*.sh` and `scripts/e2e_ssh_*.sh`

Completion evidence for any future bead under this epic should be considered incomplete unless it records exact commands, artifact paths, and enough logs to diagnose failure without rerunning locally.","created_at":"2026-04-03T02:04:25Z"},{"id":2821,"issue_id":"ft-akx00.8","author":"IndigoFox","text":"Progress update: tightened the shared placeholder-remediation verification contract in `tests/e2e/lib_rch_guards.sh` and the owned ft-akx00 harnesses for 6.2 / 6.4. The concrete changes are: 1) explicit probe/smoke log path helpers, 2) extractor helpers for selected worker / probe worker inventory / sync duration / remote duration / exit code, 3) a sentinel smoke log when `RCH_SKIP_SMOKE_PREFLIGHT=1`, and 4) owned harness summaries now record `rch_probe`, `rch_smoke`, and per-step `*.rch_meta.json` sidecars. Verified this contract concretely with `tests/e2e/test_ft_akx00_6_2_backend_resize_framebuffer.sh`, artifact root `artifacts/placeholder-remediation/ft-akx00.6.2/backend_resize_framebuffer/20260403T182413Z`.","created_at":"2026-04-03T18:28:32Z"},{"id":2822,"issue_id":"ft-akx00.8","author":"IndigoFox","text":"Follow-up validation: the tightened contract was also exercised via `tests/e2e/test_ft_akx00_6_4_terminfo_image_rendering.sh`, artifact root `artifacts/placeholder-remediation/ft-akx00.6.4/terminfo_image_rendering/20260403T182902Z`. That run produced a complete `summary.json` plus `rch_probe` / `rch_smoke` / per-step `*.rch_meta.json` sidecars even on failure. Results were 3 pass / 1 fail; the failing leg was remote clippy, and the failure was unrelated repo lint debt in `frankenterm/dynamic/derive/src/attr.rs` (`clippy::collapsible_match`), not a break in the contract plumbing itself. This gives the ft-akx00 contract both an honest failure-path example and a passing-step example with captured worker identity and durations.","created_at":"2026-04-03T18:36:45Z"},{"id":3524,"issue_id":"ft-akx00.8","author":"SunnyTiger","text":"SunnyTiger 2026-04-19: standardized the shared placeholder-remediation verification contract in repo docs. Canonical references are now `docs/test-logging-contract.md` (new `ft-akx00` profile with required JSONL fields, artifact set, and reproduction-command rules) and `docs/e2e-harness-spec.md` (new bead-scoped verification profile, required artifact root, suite map for tracks 2-7, and redaction/evidence requirements). Also extended `tests/e2e/lib_rch_guards.sh` metadata sidecars to record `repo_root`, `smoke_target_dir`, timeout settings, and whether smoke preflight was explicitly requested to be skipped, so the docs match auditable on-disk evidence instead of chat-only expectations.","created_at":"2026-04-19T14:32:54Z"}]}
 {"id":"ft-arhlm","title":"[MEDIUM] MCP missing-golden panic still points operators at the wrong regeneration command","description":"Commit f6e7cc15 corrected the drift-path regeneration command, but the missing-golden panic still tells operators to run `UPDATE_GOLDEN=1 cargo test --test mcp_manifest_golden` (`crates/frankenterm-core/tests/mcp_manifest_golden.rs:200-205`). In this workspace, default features enable a much wider surface than the minimal review lane, so regenerating via that command can produce a different manifest than the intended `--no-default-features --features mcp,asupersync-runtime` path. This leaves the test with two conflicting operator instructions and can regenerate the wrong golden when the fixture is absent. Expected fix: make the missing-file panic use the same explicit feature-set command as the drift path and top-of-file documentation.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-20T05:58:05.098815Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-20T18:12:02.105111Z","closed_at":"2026-04-20T18:12:02.104627Z","close_reason":"Already fixed — all three panic messages (header doc, missing-golden, drift) consistently use --no-default-features --features mcp,asupersync-runtime after f6e7cc15 and subsequent commits","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
-{"id":"ft-atcr0","title":"backpressure::classify silently returns Green when config thresholds are NaN","description":"# Defect

`crates/frankenterm-core/src/backpressure.rs` at `BackpressureManager::classify` (lines 416-438):

```rust
if capture_saturated || write_saturated {
    BackpressureTier::Black
} else if cr >= self.config.red_capture || wr >= self.config.red_write {
    BackpressureTier::Red
} else if cr >= self.config.yellow_capture || wr >= self.config.yellow_write {
    BackpressureTier::Yellow
} else {
    BackpressureTier::Green
}
```

Any `>=` comparison with NaN is false. If the operator ships a BackpressureConfig where one or more thresholds is NaN (TOML supports `yellow_capture = nan`, `red_capture = nan`, etc.), every branch falls through to Green. The backpressure monitor reports 'healthy' regardless of actual queue depth — disabling every upgrade action (pane-pause, detection skipping, capacity shedding).

# Parallel to ft-761tz

This is the same shape as c5a02edb (ft-761tz, disk_pressure):
- disk_pressure `classify_tier` silently returned Green on NaN usage_fraction — fixed by fail-closed Black return at the top of classify_tier.
- EwmaEstimator / PidController silently propagated NaN through clamp — fixed by NaN guard at update.

backpressure.rs has not received the same hardening. Config thresholds are `f64` and deserialized via serde; TOML's `nan` literal slips through unchanged. No `validate()` method exists on BackpressureConfig.

# Fix

1. Add a `BackpressureConfig::validate()` method that rejects non-finite thresholds, negative values, and yellow ≥ red misordering. Emit a descriptive error.
2. Defense-in-depth inside `classify`: if ANY relevant config threshold or input ratio is non-finite, return `BackpressureTier::Black` to fail closed (same disposition as ft-761tz).
3. Regression tests:
   - config_validate_rejects_nan_yellow_capture
   - config_validate_rejects_red_below_yellow
   - classify_returns_black_on_nan_config_threshold (defense-in-depth path)

# Severity

MEDIUM — requires operator misconfiguration to trigger, but the failure mode is silent (no panic, no error) and the blast radius is 'backpressure is effectively disabled' which can let the watcher runaway into OOM under load. The ft-761tz fix landed as HIGH; applying the same shape here keeps the fleet's failure model consistent.","status":"in_progress","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-22T10:03:47.755984Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-22T10:04:00.606743Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}

} else if cr >= self.config.yellow_capture || wr >= self.config.yellow_write {

 {"id":"ft-azjol","title":"[sec] ipc.rs: non-constant-time token compare (HIGH)","description":"CVSS v3.1 AV:L/AC:H/PR:L/UI:N/S:U/C:H/I:H/A:N ~5.7. Fixed in 5810a3a4. IpcAuth::authorize() used String::eq which short-circuits on first byte mismatch, plus .find() which short-circuits on first candidate match. Both are exploitable via timing side-channel over Unix socket: co-tenant process can recover the stored IPC token byte-by-byte AND distinguish token position in the vec. Fix: constant-time byte XOR accumulator in ipc_constant_time_eq + explicit loop in authorize() that visits every candidate. 7 regression tests. Found during /security-audit-for-saas adapted for CLI control-plane (pane6 phase C).","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-21T17:35:27.060545Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-21T17:35:31.327952Z","closed_at":"2026-04-21T17:35:31.327699Z","close_reason":"Fixed in 5810a3a4","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"ft-b5rbd","title":"[HIGH] GUI resize increment saturation can overflow backend min-size arithmetic","description":"Commit 193a9ebb changes `ResizeIncrementCalculator` to saturate `base_width`/`base_height` at the u16 ceiling (`crates/frankenterm-gui/src/resize_increment_calculator.rs:15-35`). Multiple window backends then immediately compute `base + increment` in u16 without guarding overflow (`frankenterm/window/src/os/macos/window.rs:1410-1412`, `frankenterm/window/src/os/x11/window.rs:1892-1893`, `frankenterm/window/src/os/wayland/window.rs:915-916`). With large padding/border/tab-bar geometry, the new saturation can produce `base_width == u16::MAX`, so `base_width + x` overflows before min-size clamping. This turns an intended overflow hardening change into a runtime panic/wraparound path on oversized layouts. Expected fix: clamp stored base dimensions to `u16::MAX - increment` (or widen backend arithmetic) and add a regression test that asserts `base + increment` stays representable.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-20T05:58:04.554054Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-20T06:42:08.945504Z","closed_at":"2026-04-20T06:42:08.945232Z","close_reason":"dup of ft-htf0i","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"ft-b677n","title":"[MEDIUM] wgpu readback poll loop has no test coverage for timeout, disconnect, or error-propagation paths","description":"Commit 6f41130c (br-43bbl fix wgpu readback polling) rewrites the
readback wait in crates/frankenterm-gui/src/termwindow/webgpu.rs:168-193
from:

\`\`\`rust
self.device.poll(PollType::Wait { submission_index: None, timeout: Some(WEBGPU_READBACK_TIMEOUT) })?;
receiver.recv_timeout(WEBGPU_READBACK_TIMEOUT)?...
\`\`\`

to a bounded poll loop:

\`\`\`rust
let deadline = Instant::now() + WEBGPU_READBACK_TIMEOUT;
loop {
    self.device.poll(wgpu::PollType::Poll)?;
    match receiver.recv_timeout(Duration::from_millis(10)) {
        Ok(result) => { result?; break; }
        Err(RecvTimeoutError::Timeout) if Instant::now() < deadline => continue,
        Err(RecvTimeoutError::Timeout) => return Err(...),
        Err(RecvTimeoutError::Disconnected) => return Err(...),
    }
}
\`\`\`

Concerns with the new implementation:

1. \`PollType::Poll\` is non-blocking by contract in wgpu 25. The loop
   relies on \`Poll\` actually driving the \`map_async\` callback forward.
   If a future wgpu point release changes \`Poll\` to be strictly
   \"dispatch-only\" (no callback invocation), the loop would always
   hit the 5s timeout. There is no test pinning this behavior.

2. The \`Disconnected\` branch is reachable only if the \`map_async\`
   closure drops without sending — that happens e.g., if wgpu drops
   the submission without invoking the callback (device lost, internal
   error). No test exercises this path.

3. \`Instant::now() + WEBGPU_READBACK_TIMEOUT\` panics on overflow
   (impossible in practice for 5s, but worth noting as a latent
   concern). Prefer \`checked_add\`.

4. Busy-wait cost: on the timeout path, the loop spins ~500 iterations
   (5000ms / 10ms) calling \`device.poll\` each time. The prior
   \`PollType::Wait\` blocked in the driver. This is a CPU-efficiency
   regression on cancellation/device-loss paths, not a correctness
   regression.

Impact: readback behavior on wgpu failure paths (device lost, long
delay, map_async disconnect) is silently unverified. A correctness
regression in \`PollType::Poll\` semantics between wgpu versions would
silently break screenshots and readback-based features.

Fix direction:
- Add a unit-level test with a mock/stub Device that controls
  \`poll()\` return timing and can force a Disconnected on the mpsc
  channel. Assert each of the four loop outcomes.
- Consider moving the readback loop into a small helper function
  that takes a \`Fn()\` poll closure + channel receiver, to make it
  unit-testable without a real GPU.
- If unit isolation is impractical, at minimum add an integration
  test that triggers a readback and asserts completion within a
  tight CPU budget (regression test for busy-wait blow-up).

Severity: medium — latent risk in a GPU-facing path that typically
works and has no automated coverage for failure modes.

Evidence: commit 6f41130c diff;
crates/frankenterm-gui/src/termwindow/webgpu.rs:168-193 at HEAD.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-20T07:25:33.419495Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-20T08:48:13.062749Z","closed_at":"2026-04-20T08:48:13.062383Z","close_reason":"Added timeout/disconnect/cancel-style readback coverage and hardened the helper deadline with checked_add","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["gpu","review","test-gap","testing","webgpu"]}

 {"id":"ft-twnmf","title":"[MEDIUM] br-43bbl WebGPU readback polling rewrite is untested at the changed timeout/callback boundary","description":"Commit 6f41130c rewrote WebGpuTexture::read() from a single wgpu::PollType::Wait call to a manual Poll loop with 10ms recv_timeout slices (crates/frankenterm-gui/src/termwindow/webgpu.rs:163-193). That changes the control flow around map_async completion, timeout expiry, and callback disconnect handling, but the test module in the same file still only covers helper routines like padded_readback_bytes_per_row and copy_padded_readback_to_image (crates/frankenterm-gui/src/termwindow/webgpu.rs:738-1003) and never exercises read() itself. This means a regression in the new bounded-poll behavior can land without any automated signal. Add a focused regression harness or extracted helper test that proves the success, timeout, and disconnected-callback cases for the new loop.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-20T06:54:02.292808Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-20T07:24:05.990393Z","closed_at":"2026-04-20T07:24:05.990145Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"ft-uqlrq","title":"[mock] search/model2vec_embedder.rs:36 embed() is a stub that always errors","description":"search/model2vec_embedder.rs:36-38 has `fn embed(&self, _text: &str) -> Result<Vec<f32>, EmbedError> { // Stub: real implementation loads ONNX model; Err(EmbedError::ModelNotFound(...)) }`. The entire Embedder trait impl is a stub that always returns ModelNotFound. Zero production callers exist — tests in search/mod.rs and daemon/worker.rs explicitly verify model2vec is "retired" and rejected. Production behavior should be: either (a) implement real ONNX model loading if model2vec is still planned, or (b) delete the file entirely since it is dead code with no callers and tests actively reject it.","status":"closed","priority":3,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-21T17:32:43.678300Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-21T17:52:17.389463Z","closed_at":"2026-04-21T17:52:17.389208Z","close_reason":"Shipped in af925d26. Audit confirmed the file was orphaned:
- not declared in search/mod.rs (no 'mod model2vec_embedder;' anywhere)
- zero constructors of Model2VecEmbedder outside the file itself
- three tests across the tree explicitly verify model2vec is RETIRED (never advertised as a tier, rejected as a selector, rejected by the embed worker): search::tests::advertised_embedder_tiers_never_reports_retired_model2vec_backend, search::daemon::worker::tests::build_embedder_rejects_retired_model2vec_selector, tests/semantic_backend_selection_integration.rs::embed_worker_rejects_retired_model2vec_selector

Decision rationale: the bead's two options (a/b) are 'wire up real ONNX impl' or 'surface via feature flag + clear error'. Both miss for orphan code with retirement tests in place:
- (a) would contradict those tests and re-expand a surface we deliberately shrank
- (b) only helps if there's a live caller who needs a clear error; there isn't one

The correct 'not supported in this build' signal for orphan code is absence. Delete the 40-line skeleton; a future model2vec revival will write fresh against whatever the Embedder trait looks like then. Also updated the stale 'e.g. Model2Vec distilled' comment on ManagedEmbedderStack.fast in orchestrator.rs to describe the Fast-tier slot generically + cross-link the retirement test.

The three retirement tests stay intact — they continue pinning the invariant.

Verification (rch exec -- env CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/tmp/ft-p5-orch-target):
- cargo check -p frankenterm-core --lib --no-default-features: clean
- --features semantic-search build blocked by 13 pre-existing unrelated errors in web/sse.rs (select! macro fallout from the 9e1bd63b cfg-strip); my patch only removes a file + edits a doc comment so it cannot introduce compile errors

Note: the commit that carries the change is misleadingly titled 'docs(readme): correct runtime_compat boundary claim' — another agent's linter bundled my uncommitted model2vec deletion + orchestrator comment into its own commit. The 44 lines of change are correct (40 deletions in model2vec_embedder.rs + 4 lines in orchestrator.rs doc comment); only the commit title under-describes the scope.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"ft-v0339","title":"[MEDIUM] integration_mux_checkpoint_topology post-rollback check only compares pane-id set, not tree shape","description":"Commit 30172171 fixed ft-olq2e by routing the post-rollback check through server.registry().snapshot() instead of the vacuous re-deserialization of initial_topo_json. However the new primary assertion at integration_mux_checkpoint_topology.rs still only compares PANE IDs:

  post_rollback_pane_ids_sorted == baseline_pane_ids

TopologySnapshot (crates/frankenterm-core/src/session_topology.rs) is a tree of windows → tabs → panes, with per-window position/size/title and per-tab pane layout metadata. The registry snapshot, filtered to LifecycleEntityKind::Pane, flattens all of that to a bag of pane u64 ids.

Consequence: a RemoteRequest::Rollback that restores the correct set of pane entities but (a) reparents them under the wrong tab, (b) drops a window or tab node, (c) corrupts the split tree under a tab, or (d) restores panes in the wrong order will still pass the assertion. Rollback tree-shape correctness is therefore still not covered by this test — the bead it closes was motivated precisely by false confidence in rollback behavior.

Expected fix:
- Reconstruct a post-rollback TopologySnapshot from the live server (registry + whatever stores tab/window edges) and compare it field-by-field against the pre-expansion baseline — at least window ids + tab ids + pane ids grouped by tab.
- Keep the pane-id-set assertion as a weaker redundant guard.

Regression risk: medium. The test name promises topology validation; reviewers relying on this bead's closure will assume tree-structure regressions are caught when in fact only pane existence is.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-20T07:02:21.495704Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-20T08:19:22.017561Z","closed_at":"2026-04-20T08:19:22.017321Z","close_reason":"Strengthened rollback validation to require exact TopologySnapshot restoration, not just pane-id/fingerprint agreement","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["mux","review","session-persistence","test-gap"]}

 {"id":"ft-w4znq","title":"error renderer maps typed pane-not-found to internal error","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","assignee":"pane8","created_at":"2026-04-22T09:43:19.139058Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-22T09:55:49.736016Z","closed_at":"2026-04-22T09:55:49.735721Z","close_reason":"mapping landed in bb844a72 while this audit was in flight; no remaining code diff under this bead","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"ft-wajba","title":"tmux cmd_queue drop-head-in-flight corrupts Guarded response routing","description":"frankenterm/mux/src/tmux.rs:73-91 (push_command_capped) vs :165-186 (Guarded response handler).

push_command_capped(queue, cmd) does queue.drain(..excess) when queue exceeds CMD_QUEUE_MAX_DEPTH=50_000. The drain is from the FRONT. But when TmuxDomainState.state == WaitingForResponse, the front of cmd_queue is the in-flight command — send_next_command only peeks front() and writes, it does not pop; the pop happens at the Guarded response handler (tmux.rs:172) via cmd_queue.pop_front().

Race / correctness gap: if push_command_capped runs while state == WaitingForResponse and queue.len() reaches 50_001, drain drops the in-flight head. When the Guarded response arrives, pop_front() pops a DIFFERENT pending command and calls cmd.process_result(domain_id, &response) on the wrong command — cross-wiring response payload onto the wrong command parser. Both threads hold the cmd_queue lock so the drain+pop ordering is deterministic under the lock, but the correctness violation stands.

Fix options (not applied here, pattern-level):
 1. When state == WaitingForResponse, skip index 0 in drain: queue.drain(1..=excess) (but preserves head).
 2. Drain from the back (newest-first) which discards most recently enqueued layout/session events that often re-fire.
 3. Abort the in-flight state machine (set state=Idle) and drain — heavy-handed but restores invariant.

Only triggers in pathological event storms (remote tmux lag + 50_000 queued commands) so severity is MEDIUM. The original bug fixed in 1055cffc (push-site cap enforcement) traded unbounded memory for this subtle correctness edge. A regression test would drive tmux into WaitingForResponse, push >50_001 Layout/SessionChanged events, then fire a Guarded response and assert process_result is NOT called against a different command type than the one that was sent.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-21T17:16:32.972876Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-21T17:34:41.760204Z","closed_at":"2026-04-21T17:34:41.759836Z","close_reason":"Shipped in 9cc7e6c2. push_command_capped migrated from free function to TmuxDomainState method so it can consult self.state before choosing drain range:
- state == WaitingForResponse → drain(1..=excess) (preserves index 0, the in-flight cmd awaiting its Guarded response)
- any other state → drain(..excess) (retains existing drop-oldest semantics)

Both drain branches log distinctly so event-storm incidents are traceable. Lock ordering matches Guarded-handler's (cmd_queue first, state transiently inside method): no deadlock. send_next_command's cap-drain is untouched — already guarded by early 'state != Idle → return'.

Regression coverage: 2 new tests under mux::tmux::tests that each seat a distinguishable SplitPane at index 0 and push CMD_QUEUE_MAX_DEPTH more commands; one flips state to WaitingForResponse and asserts the head's get_command() text is unchanged (pre-fix fails), the other keeps state Idle and asserts the head IS drained (pins against future over-correction). Existing cmd_queue_hard_cap_drains_oldest_when_exceeded still green after the refactor.

Verification via rch exec -- env CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/tmp/ft-p5-orch-target:
- cargo check -p mux --lib: clean
- cargo test -p mux --lib cmd_queue: 3/3 green.

Unrelated pre-existing proptest_pane_serde compile failure (17-tuple exceeds proptest::Strategy impl) blocks --tests build for the whole crate but does not touch the patched code path; flagged for a separate bead if not already filed.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"ft-wk5fo","title":"move FTS5 indexing off the capture-write transaction","description":"# Background

Follow-up to ft-kcsae (answered). Live capture path in storage.rs
writes synchronously INTO the FTS5 index via three AFTER-triggers:

    storage.rs:169  output_segments_ai AFTER INSERT  → INSERT INTO output_segments_fts
    storage.rs:173  output_segments_ad AFTER DELETE  → fts delete
    storage.rs:177  output_segments_au AFTER UPDATE  → fts delete + insert

Every captured segment pays the inverted-index rebuild cost before
`append_segment_sync` returns (storage.rs:12308 → 12335 INSERT INTO
output_segments). Under 200-pane fanout the trigger cost is paid 200x
per-wire-tick.

# Existing primitives that can be reused

- `fts_pane_progress` table (storage.rs:472) — already tracks per-pane
  last-indexed-seq with resumable offsets.
- `sync_fts_on_startup(conn, FtsSyncConfig)` (storage.rs:16131) — the
  batched catchup engine. Currently only fires at startup.
- `FtsSyncConfig` (storage.rs:2363) — batch_size=100, max_batch_bytes=1
  MB, commit_progress per batch. Ready knobs.

# Proposal

1. Drop the three AFTER triggers on output_segments.
2. Add a writer-thread ticker (every N seconds OR after M segments
   since last catchup) that runs `sync_fts_on_startup` with the
   existing FtsSyncConfig and uses `fts_pane_progress` to pick up
   where it left off.
3. Gate behind a `storage.fts_indexing_mode = 'sync' | 'deferred'`
   config flag; default 'sync' at first, flip to 'deferred' after a
   round of search-freshness observability.
4. Expose catchup-lag (now - max(fts_pane_progress.last_indexed_at))
   as a metric so operators can spot index drift.

# Risk

- Search freshness becomes eventual (default lag = catchup interval).
- FTS rows can briefly diverge from output_segments during a crash
  between INSERT and the next catchup tick — acceptable because
  `sync_fts_on_startup` already handles gap recovery on startup.

# Measurement prerequisite

Before landing: add a bench that measures `append_segment_sync` p99
under a 200-pane load with FTS5 enabled (current) vs. triggers
disabled + deferred catchup. If the gap is not material, don't ship —
the synchronous path is simpler and the trigger tax might be in the
noise given SQLite's WAL + page cache.","status":"in_progress","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","owner":"pane3-fts5-scaffold","created_at":"2026-04-21T18:00:59.588862Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-21T20:17:34.803115Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"comments":[{"id":3602,"issue_id":"ft-wk5fo","author":"pane3-fts5-scaffold","text":"Claimed for scaffolding. Build verification currently blocked by ft-h9g0q (non-exhaustive match on new Error variants in error_renderer.rs:34 + retry.rs:378). Just shipped a mechanical fix in commit 87d4546b covering all 3 new typed variants; waiting on cold cargo check to confirm green. Once compile is green, scope for this bead's first shippable slice is the criterion benchmark for append_segment_sync p99 described in the ticket's 'Measurement prerequisite' — no implementation changes before we have numbers.","created_at":"2026-04-21T20:17:34Z"}]}
